title: TLS, Virtual Hosts, Credentials, and Authorization · Runtime Contract: Vhost Resource Permissions
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must verify server identity, roots, client credentials, vhost permissions, rotation, redaction, and tenant abuse boundaries; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the vhost resource permissions increment in a Go TLS and least-privilege connection contract. Learners…
objectives: Grant minimum configure, write, and read regex permissions per virtual host and separately evaluate topic authorization; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Choose generated credentials, x509 EXTERNAL, or OAuth 2 identity with explicit broker, client, token, renewal, and revocation support; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Vhost Resource Permissions · Theory Vhost Resource Permissions
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 1890 during Go publisher supervisor losing one state notification. Build deterministic Go evidence to verify TLS and vhost identity, redact secrets, and compute least-privilege permissions; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then cancel the parent context while a result is still ambiguous. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Grant minimum configure, write, and read regex permissions per virtual host and separately evaluate topic authorization; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
